subject: History (Our Pasts - II)
chapter: Tracing Changes Through a Thousand Years

activities_projects.title: Epistemological Shift in Cartography
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the impact of Eurocentrism and maritime technology on geography
materials: High-resolution images of Map 1 and Map 2
steps: Deconstruct why Al-Idrisi used an inverted perspective (South at top) and its roots in Arab geographical traditions
Contrast this with the 18th-century French map used by the 'Atlas Nouveau'
Evaluate how 'scientific' accuracy in the second map was a byproduct of colonial and commercial exploration needs
Write a thesis on how maps are not neutral but are 'socially constructed' by the cartographer's context
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critically evaluates how power and utility dictate the representation of geographical space
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis regarding the intent and cultural context of map-makers (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Semantics of 'Hindustan': A Political Inquiry
activities_projects.objective: Trace the territorial and cultural fluidity of national identity
materials: Excerpts from Minhaj-i-Siraj and Amir Khusrau
steps: Compare Minhaj-i-Siraj’s political definition (Punjab, Haryana, lands between Ganga-Yamuna) with Babur's cultural-geographical definition
Analyze why Minhaj-i-Siraj specifically excluded South India from his definition
Investigate how the 14th-century term 'Hind' differed from the 13th-century 'Hindustan'
Draft a paper on the dangers of 'Anachronism'—applying modern borders to medieval terminology
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Internalizes the complexity of historical geography and the evolution of the concept of India
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical consistency in explaining the shifting borders of political terminology (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Textual Criticism and Manuscript Mutation
activities_projects.objective: Understand the methodology of 'Philology' in historical research
materials: Two versions of a text with subtle variations
steps: Analyze the two versions of Ziyauddin Barani’s 'Tarikh-i Firoz Shahi' (1356 vs 1358)
Explain the 'Scribal error' phenomenon and how it transforms an original manuscript over centuries
Discuss the challenges historians face when the 'archetype' (original) is lost and only varied copies remain
Create a 'Stemmatic' diagram showing how errors propagate in manual copying
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies critical thinking to assess the reliability and authenticity of textual sources
activities_projects.evaluation: Technical understanding of manuscript variance and Barani's revisionist history (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Socio-Technological Synthesis (700-1750)
activities_projects.objective: Link technological diffusion to demographic and economic shifts
materials: Research data on the Persian Wheel and Fire-arms
steps: Research the origins of the Persian Wheel (Saqiya) and its impact on the agricultural expansion into forested zones
Analyze the correlation between the 'Clearing of Forests' and the social transformation of forest-dwellers into peasants
Explain how the Spinning Wheel changed the economic status of weaver communities
Present a case for the medieval period being an 'Age of Innovation' rather than a 'Dark Age'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Correlates environmental changes with the emergence of new social classes and peasant societies
activities_projects.evaluation: Ability to link technological arrival with specific social outcomes (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Jati Hierarchies and Local Autonomy
activities_projects.objective: Investigate the interaction between self-governing Jatis and State authority
materials: Sociological case studies on Jati Panchayats
steps: Analyze the statement 'ranks were not fixed permanently' in the Jati system during this period
Investigate how 'Jati Panchayats' functioned as a proto-legal system independent of the King’s courts
Explain the 'Interlocking Relationship' between Jati rules and Village (Gram) administration
Discuss how the accumulation of 'Power and Wealth' allowed certain Jatis to move up the social ladder
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes the non-static and decentralized nature of medieval Indian social structures
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity in explaining social mobility and local judicial autonomy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Political Economy of Patronage
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the symbiotic relationship between Brahmanas and emerging dynasties
steps: Research why 'New Monarchs' (like Rajputs) required the validation of Brahmanas
Evaluate how the mastery of Sanskrit served as 'Cultural Capital' for the priestly class
Analyze the impact of 'Land Grants' on the economic power and prestige of temples
Argue how patronage transformed Brahmanas into a dominant socio-political force
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Synthesizes the link between religious legitimacy, linguistic monopoly, and political consolidation
activities_projects.evaluation: Critical analysis of the social contract between the ruler and the scholar (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Islamic Legal Schools and Indian Integration
activities_projects.objective: Study the intellectual diversity within medieval Islam in India
materials: Comparative notes on Hanafi and Shafi'i schools
steps: Distinguish between the theology of Shia and Sunni sects in the Indian context
Investigate the Hanafi and Shafi’i schools of law and their influence on the Delhi Sultanate
Explain the role of the 'Ulama' in advising the state on Sharia and governance
Discuss how Islam adapted to the pluralistic environment of the subcontinent
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the legal and theological diversity within Islam and its institutional role in India
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy in distinguishing legal schools and their impact on state policy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Bhakti: A Deconstruction of Ritualism
activities_projects.objective: Analyze Bhakti as an egalitarian response to social stratification
steps: Critique the 'Temple-Priest' monopoly on salvation during the 7th-14th centuries
Explain how the Bhakti concept of a 'Personal God' democratized spirituality
Analyze why the Bhakti movement coincided with the rise of lower-caste social movements
Discuss the long-term impact of Bhakti on the breakdown of rigid caste barriers in worship
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes Bhakti as a subaltern movement that challenged traditional religious authority
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of reflection on the social implications of personal devotion (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Historiographical Critique of Periodization
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the bias in James Mill’s tripartite division of history
materials: James Mill’s 'The History of British India' excerpts
steps: Deconstruct the 'Communal' basis of Mill’s division (Hindu, Muslim, British)
Analyze why modern historians prefer 'Ancient, Medieval, Modern' over religious labels
Explain the economic and social continuities that make the 'Medieval' label problematic
Write a critique on how periodization can be used as a tool for colonial justification
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops a critical understanding of how the 'naming' of time influences historical narrative
activities_projects.evaluation: Strength of the historiographical argument against Mill's periodization (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Paper Revolution' and Archival Statecraft
activities_projects.objective: Trace the impact of affordable writing material on governance
materials: Data on paper production and manuscript availability
steps: Analyze how the plummeting price of paper in the 13th century led to a 'Textual Explosion'
Investigate how this shift allowed for more complex administrative records (Farmans, Petitions)
Explain the concept of an 'Archive' as a repository of state power and memory
Discuss why oral traditions began to lose primacy to written records during this millennium
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects material technology (paper) to the growth of the bureaucratic and archival state
activities_projects.evaluation: Logic in linking technological change to administrative complexity (10 marks)
